L1.area: Core protocol handling and message processing infrastructure

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists where a sparse lookup table used for converting protocol message identifiers to human-readable names does not have entries for all valid identifiers within the accepted range. When an identifier without a corresponding table entry is encountered, a null pointer is returned and immediately dereferenced without validation, causing a crash. This can be triggered remotely without authentication.

L3.area: Protocol command and reply message name resolution utilities
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the reply name resolution logic where a sparse lookup table does not define entries for all valid reply identifiers in the accepted range. When a valid but undefined reply identifier is looked up, the table returns a null pointer which is immediately dereferenced without a null check, causing a denial of service. An attacker can trigger this remotely without establishing a secure connection.

ground_truth.reason: In the osdp_reply_name function, the names lookup table does not define entries for all valid reply IDs in the REPLY_ACK to REPLY_XRD range. When an undefined but in-range reply ID is looked up, the returned null pointer is dereferenced without a null check, causing a crash.
